CVE-2016-6321.title: Directory traversal vulnerability in the safer_name_suffix functi ...
CVE-2016-6321.description: Directory traversal vulnerability in the safer_name_suffix function in GNU tar 1.14 through 1.29 might allow remote attackers to bypass an intended protection mechanism and write to arbitrary files via vectors related to improper sanitization of the file_name parameter, aka POINTYFEATHER.

CVE-2018-20482.title: GNU Tar through 1.30, when --sparse is used, mishandles file shri ...
CVE-2018-20482.description: GNU Tar through 1.30, when --sparse is used, mishandles file shrinkage during read access, which allows local users to cause a denial of service (infinite read loop in sparse_dump_region in sparse.c) by modifying a file that is supposed to be archived by a different user's process (e.g., a system backup running as root).

CVE-2019-9923.title: pax_decode_header in sparse.c in GNU Tar before 1.32 had a NULL p ...
CVE-2019-9923.description: pax_decode_header in sparse.c in GNU Tar before 1.32 had a NULL pointer dereference when parsing certain archives that have malformed extended headers.

CVE-2022-48303.title: GNU Tar through 1.34 has a one-byte out-of-bounds read that resul ...
CVE-2022-48303.description: GNU Tar through 1.34 has a one-byte out-of-bounds read that results in use of uninitialized memory for a conditional jump. Exploitation to change the flow of control has not been demonstrated. The issue occurs in from_header in list.c via a V7 archive in which mtime has approximately 11 whitespace characters.

CVE-2026-5704.title: A flaw was found in tar. A remote attacker could exploit this vul ...
CVE-2026-5704.description: A flaw was found in tar. A remote attacker could exploit this vulnerability by crafting a malicious archive, leading to hidden file injection with fully attacker-controlled content. This bypasses pre-extraction inspection mechanisms, potentially allowing an attacker to introduce malicious files onto a system without detection.
